title: hiraea andersonii
intro: Hiraea andersonii is a species of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae, commonly known as the forest mint or mint tree. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of hiraea andersonii include relief from fever, headache, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce pain. Hiraea andersonii can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves can also be used in cooking as a flavoring agent, similar to mint.
